meaning_english:
If the reporting verb is followed by an object, 'say to' changes to 'tell' and 'said to' changes to 'told'.

• Pattern: Said to ➔ Told; Say to ➔ Tell.
• Note: If there is no object, 'said' remains 'said'.
• Example: He said to me, 'I am ready.' ➔ He told me that he was ready.

If the Reporting Verb is in the Past Tense, the Tense of the reported speech changes as follows:

• Simple Present ➔ Simple Past
• Present Continuous ➔ Past Continuous
• Present Perfect ➔ Past Perfect
• Simple Past ➔ Past Perfect
• Tip: No change if the reporting verb is in Present or Future tense.

Pronouns change according to the SON rule:
• 1st Person (I, We) ➔ Subject of Reporting Verb
• 2nd Person (You) ➔ Object of Reporting Verb
• 3rd Person (He, She, It, They) ➔ No Change.
• Direct: She said to me, 'You are late.' ➔ Indirect: She told me that I was late.

Modals change into their past forms in Indirect Speech:

• Will/Shall ➔ Would
• Can ➔ Could
• May ➔ Might
• Must ➔ Had to (or remains Must).
• Direct: He said, 'I can swim.' ➔ Indirect: He said that he could swim.

meaning_kannada: ಜಗತ್ತಿನ ಸತ್ಯ ಸಂಗತಿಗಳು ಅಥವಾ ಅಭ್ಯಾಸಗಳ ನಿಯಮ.
meaning_english:
If the reported speech expresses a Universal Truth, Habitual Fact, or Historical Fact, the tense DOES NOT change even if the reporting verb is in the past.

• Direct: Teacher said, 'The sun rises in the east.' ➔ Indirect: Teacher said that the sun rises in the east.

meaning_kannada: ಸಮಯ ಮತ್ತು ದೂರವನ್ನು ಸೂಚಿಸುವ ಪದಗಳ ಬದಲಾವಣೆ.
meaning_english:
Words expressing nearness are changed into words expressing distance:

• Now ➔ Then
• This ➔ That
• Here ➔ There
• Today ➔ That day
• Tomorrow ➔ The next day / The following day
• Yesterday ➔ The previous day / The day before.

For questions that start with a helping verb (Is, Do, Have, Can), use 'if' or 'whether' as a conjunction.

• Pattern: Said/Said to ➔ Asked.
• Note: Remove the question mark and change the structure to assertive.
• Direct: He said, 'Are you coming?' ➔ Indirect: He asked if I was coming.

If the question starts with a Wh-word (Who, What, Why, Where), no conjunction (like that or if) is used. The Wh-word itself acts as the connector.

• Direct: She asked, 'Where is your house?' ➔ Indirect: She asked where my house was.

Reporting verb changes to Ordered, Commanded, Advised, or Requested. Use 'to' + V1 as the connector.

• Direct: The boss said, 'Work hard.' ➔ Indirect: The boss ordered to work hard.
• Negative: 'Don't' changes to 'Not to'.

Reporting verb changes to 'Exclaimed with joy/sorrow/wonder'. Remove exclamatory marks and use 'that'.

• Alas! ➔ Exclaimed with sorrow.
• Hurrah! ➔ Exclaimed with joy.
• Direct: He said, 'Alas! I am undone.' ➔ Indirect: He exclaimed with sorrow that he was undone.
